text: The surgeon uses diathermy to superficially mark the mucosal bridges on the tissue. An injection of adrenaline in saline is administered to reduce bleeding, aiding in the precise dissection of the internal and external sphincters. A V-shaped incision is made at the mucocutaneous border, followed by careful dissection of the pile using diathermy up to two centimeters above the dentate line, without ligating the vascular pedicle.

The surgeon completes the excision of the first pile, revealing the bright white internal sphincter, indicating correct dissection with the monopolar scalpel. The same technique is applied to the remaining two piles.

The surgeon uses an Eisenhammer anal retractor to achieve clear visualization of the internal sphincter throughout the operation. The procedure concludes with the removal of a residual skin tag and verification of the mucocutaneous bridges' integrity, ensuring no internal sphincterotomy is performed.
